zh.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton於大約1550年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾賀普頓斯塔爾。她於大約1570年與John Farrar在英國藍卡爾波利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1596年10月9日在英國藍卡爾波利享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾吉可哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton于大约1550年出生在英国英格兰越可爾賀普頓斯塔爾。她于大约1570年与John Farrar在英国藍卡爾波利结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1596年10月9日在英国藍卡爾波利享年47岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾吉可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton was born about 1550, in Heptonstall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married John Farrar about 1570, in Burnley, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 9 October 1596, in Burnley, Lancashire, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton est née environ 1550, à Heptonstall, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé John Farrar environ 1570, à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 octobre 1596, à Burnley, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterrée à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton wurde um 1550 in Heptonstall, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1570 in Burnley, Lancashire, England John Farrar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. Oktober 1596 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Burnley, Lancashire, England und wurde in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton è nata il circa 1550 nel Heptonstall, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato John Farrar circa 1570, nel Burnley, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 9 ottobre 1596, nel Burnley, Lancashire, England, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolta a Kirkham, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghtonは約1550年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアヘプトンスタルで生まれた。約1570年にイギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーでJohn Farrarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1596年10月9日にイギリスランカシュアイアブルンリーで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアキルクハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton(은)는 대략 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 헤프토스탤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1570년 영국 랭카실르 번리에서 John Farrar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1596년 10월 9일 영국 랭카실르 번리에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 커카에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton nasceu aproximadamente 1550, em Heptonstall, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com John Farrar aproximadamente 1570, em Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de outubro de 1596, em Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultada em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton родилась около 1550 г. в местности хептонсталл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Farrar около 1570 г. в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 9 октября 1596 г. в местности бурнли, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и была похоронена в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Isabell Houghton nació aproximadamente 1550, en Heptonstall, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con John Farrar aproximadamente 1570, en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 9 de octubre de 1596, en Burnley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultada en Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
